overview: Build a Dockerized, single-user web application that records and replays web navigation workflows inside a real, visible Chromium browser. The product must capture browser actions and runtime artifacts such as cookies and clipboard history, then produce a structured JSON result with strong debug visibility when a run fails.
goals: Record realistic browser workflows in a visible Chromium session, including navigation, tab lifecycle, clicks, text input, shortcuts, selection, clipboard, and scroll actions.
Replay recorded workflows on demand in the same visible Chromium session with normal, slow, and step-through modes.
Capture cookies and clipboard artifacts during runs and turn them into a structured output JSON with configurable filtering and templating.
Provide step-level debug visibility with statuses, failure reason, timeline, screenshots, and browser context so failures are actionable.
Run as a self-contained Dockerized web app with an architecture that can grow toward DOM extraction, network traces, conditions, loops, and multi-workflow orchestration.
nonGoals: Multi-user accounts, RBAC, or team collaboration in MVP.
Support for browsers other than Chromium in MVP.
Parallel workflow runs or parallel browser sessions in MVP.
Network HAR capture, full tracing, or advanced traffic interception in MVP.
Conditions, loops, branching logic, or cross-workflow orchestration in MVP.
Guaranteed drag-and-drop recording in MVP if reliability is poor; the data model should remain extensible for V2.
successMetrics: A workflow with 20 or fewer steps can be recorded and replayed in the same environment with at least 90% success after selector stabilization.
A failed run exposes the failing step, selector, URL, tab/frame context, and technical reason within 30 seconds of opening the run viewer.
A user can export the final structured JSON result in one click after a successful run.
A fresh environment can reach a visible Chromium session from `docker compose up` in under 10 minutes following project documentation.
Scheduled runs show last run, next run, and success or failure history without requiring direct database access.
openQuestions: Should drag-and-drop recording be part of MVP if Playwright support proves stable, or explicitly deferred to V2?
Is the visible browser surface acceptable as an embedded noVNC session inside the web UI, or is a host-native Chromium window required later?
Should secrets for masked cookies be encrypted with a user-supplied key, or is a local app-managed key acceptable for single-user MVP?
Do we need to support clipboard entries beyond plain text in MVP, such as HTML fragments or images, or is MIME metadata enough initially?
How much cross-origin iframe interaction is expected in target workflows, given highlighting and selector recovery limitations?
assumptions: This is a new project starting from an empty repository.
The product is single-user and local or self-hosted, so MVP does not require authentication.
A visible Chromium browser inside Docker will be exposed through an embedded browser viewer backed by Xvfb and noVNC.
Playwright is the browser automation layer for recording metadata and replay execution.
SQLite is sufficient for MVP persistence and fits the single-user deployment model.
constraints: The application must run in Docker.
Chromium must remain visibly accessible during recording and replay.
The MVP must preserve an extensible architecture for V2 additions such as DOM extraction, network traces, conditions, loops, and multi-workflow support.

personas.name: Operator
personas.description: A single technical user who records, replays, debugs, and schedules browser workflows to extract structured outputs from websites.

stack.framework: React 19 + Vite 7 frontend, Express 5 backend, TypeScript throughout, npm workspaces
stack.hosting: Single Docker container running Node 22, Chromium, Xvfb, and noVNC
stack.auth: None in MVP; single local operator session
stack.automation: Playwright controlling a persistent Chromium context
stack.realtime: WebSocket channel for browser session state, recorder events, and live run timeline updates

routes.name: Dashboard
routes.purpose: List workflows, show browser session state, and summarize recent and scheduled runs.

routes.name: New Workflow
routes.purpose: Create a workflow with metadata, start URL, cookie presets, and output rules.

routes.name: Workflow Detail
routes.purpose: Inspect recorded steps, edit workflow configuration, and manage schedule, cookies, and output mapping.

routes.name: Recorder
routes.purpose: Start a visible Chromium session and capture user actions into workflow steps.

routes.name: Replay
routes.purpose: Execute a workflow in the visible Chromium browser with speed controls and recovery actions.

routes.name: Run Viewer
routes.purpose: Inspect timeline, failure diagnostics, cookies, clipboard artifacts, and structured output for a specific run.

routes.name: Settings
routes.purpose: Show environment, secret-handling, browser, and storage configuration relevant to the single-user instance.

uiNotes: Recorder and Replay routes use a split layout with the visible browser taking at least 60% of horizontal space and an inspector panel for steps and controls.
The app shell includes workflow navigation, browser session badge, last run status, and clear empty/loading/error states on every route.
Run Viewer presents a chronological timeline with color-coded statuses, sticky diagnostics pane, and artifact tabs for cookies, clipboard, console logs, and DOM snapshots.
Replay controls must make mode obvious: normal, slow, paused, and step-through should have distinct states and disabled rules.
Sensitive cookie values are always redacted in UI, exports, and logs.
Destructive actions such as deleting a workflow or clearing stored cookies require explicit confirmation.

importFormat.description: Workflow definitions should be importable and exportable as JSON so a recorded workflow can be persisted, inspected, and replayed deterministically.

rules: MVP supports exactly one active Chromium session and one active workflow run at a time.
Selectors should prefer stable attributes first: data-testid, accessible role or label, semantic text, then CSS fallback.
If an action triggers navigation, replay must wait for navigation completion before advancing to the next step unless the step explicitly opts out.
Masked cookie values are never rendered in plaintext in UI, logs, screenshots metadata, or exported JSON.
Clipboard retention and mapping rules are evaluated in captured order and produce a final JSON object by default.
When a run fails, screenshot capture is mandatory; HTML snapshots and console diagnostics are required unless browser-level errors make them unavailable.
If a scheduled run fires while another run is active, the new run is skipped and logged rather than queued in MVP.
All timestamps should be stored in UTC ISO 8601 format.
The workflow schema must remain forward-compatible with future action types such as drag-and-drop, conditions, loops, and network events.

stories.title: Scaffold the workspace and Docker-first tooling
stories.description: As a developer, I want a new TypeScript workspace with frontend, backend, and Docker entrypoints so the product can be built incrementally without guesswork.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an npm workspace with `apps/web`, `apps/server`, and `packages/shared`, plus root scripts `npm run dev`, `npm run build`, `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test`.

Document explicit initial install commands in the repo setup, including `npm install react react-dom express zod` and `npm install -D typescript vite vitest eslint @types/node @types/react @types/react-dom concurrently`.
Add a Dockerfile and `compose.yml` that boot the app stack with placeholder Chromium system dependencies and a health endpoint contract for later browser stories.
Example: `npm run dev` brings up a visible web shell and `/api/health` returns HTTP 200 with JSON `{ "status": "ok" }`.
Negative case: when optional env files are missing, the app boots with sane single-user defaults and surfaces a clear configuration warning instead of crashing.

stories.title: Add SQLite persistence and domain schema
stories.description: As a developer, I want a durable schema for workflows, runs, cookies, clipboard entries, and schedules so the app state survives restarts and supports debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: Install persistence packages with explicit commands `npm install @prisma/client better-sqlite3` and `npm install -D prisma`.
Create a Prisma schema and initial migration for Workflow, WorkflowStep, WorkflowRun, RunStepLog, CookieArtifact, ClipboardEntry, and Schedule with enums for action types and run statuses.
Provide server-side repository methods for CRUD and list queries required by the dashboard, workflow detail pages, and run viewer.
Example: creating a workflow named `Invoice capture` stores a workflow row and can be fetched again after a process restart.
Negative case: an invalid step action type or orphaned child record is rejected by validation and does not reach the database.

stories.title: Create the app shell and route skeleton
stories.description: As the operator, I want a stable web shell with clear routes and empty states so I can navigate the product before deeper features are implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement routes `/`, `/workflows/new`, `/workflows/:workflowId`, `/workflows/:workflowId/record`, `/workflows/:workflowId/replay`, `/runs/:runId`, and `/settings`.
Build a single-user layout with top bar, left navigation, page title, route-level loading states, empty states, and route-level error handling.
Include reusable UI primitives for workflow cards, run status badges, browser session badge, and a split-pane container that later recorder and replay screens can reuse.
Example: visiting `/` with no workflows shows an empty-state CTA to create the first workflow.
Negative case: visiting `/workflows/unknown` renders a not-found state with a recovery link instead of a blank page or uncaught error.

stories.title: Launch and display a headful Chromium session
stories.description: As the operator, I want a real, visible Chromium browser in the app so I can record and replay workflows against the actual rendered site.
acceptanceCriteria: Install browser runtime packages with explicit commands `npm install playwright ws @novnc/novnc`.
Launch a single persistent Chromium context in headful mode inside the container using Xvfb and noVNC, and expose it through a visible embedded viewer on the Recorder and Replay routes.
Provide session lifecycle APIs or WebSocket events for start, stop, status, current URL, active tab count, and busy or idle state.
Example: starting a browser session from `/workflows/:workflowId/record` opens a visible Chromium window showing `about:blank` inside the viewer container.
Negative case: a second start request while a session is already active returns a `busy` state and leaves the existing session untouched.

stories.title: Create workflow CRUD and configuration editing
stories.description: As the operator, I want to create and edit workflows with configuration metadata so recording and replay have a durable place to live.
acceptanceCriteria: Add create, edit, and delete actions for workflow metadata including name, description, start URL, cookie presets, output template summary, and schedule summary.
Hydrate `/workflows/:workflowId` from SQLite and save edits through the backend instead of transient browser-only state.
Render sections for metadata, recorder status, replay settings, cookies, clipboard output rules, and schedule overview even when some sections are initially empty.
Example: saving a workflow named `Copy tracking number` with start URL `https://example.com/login` persists and remains visible after page refresh.
Negative case: blank names, invalid URLs, or duplicate workflow names surface inline validation errors and do not persist partial records.

stories.title: Record navigation and tab lifecycle steps
stories.description: As the operator, I want navigation and tab actions captured automatically so multi-page and multi-tab workflows can be replayed faithfully.
acceptanceCriteria: Recorder captures `navigate`, `goBack`, `goForward`, `reload`, `openTab`, `closeTab`, and `switchTab` steps with URL, tab reference, timestamps, and wait metadata when relevant.
Persist recorded steps in order for the active workflow and reflect them live in the step list UI during an active recording session.
Use stable tab references so replay can correlate multi-tab actions even when tab indices change between runs.
Example: opening a new tab, visiting `https://news.ycombinator.com`, then closing it produces three ordered steps tied to the same tab reference.
Negative case: transient extension or devtools targets are ignored and logged as unsupported instead of polluting the workflow definition.

stories.title: Record pointer, field, and scroll interactions
stories.description: As the operator, I want meaningful on-page interactions recorded so replay can reproduce user behavior beyond simple navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Recorder captures click, double-click, optional context-menu trigger, focus, blur, change, input, and scroll actions with selector, coordinates when needed, and field metadata where safe.
Generate selectors using a preference order of `data-testid`, accessible role or label, semantic text, then CSS fallback, and store the selector strategy used.
Debounce noisy DOM events so common text entry does not create redundant change steps.
Example: clicking a submit button and typing into an email field creates focused, replayable steps without manual cleanup.
Negative case: hidden, disabled, or detached elements encountered during capture are skipped with a warning instead of recording unusable selectors.

stories.title: Record keyboard shortcuts, selection, and clipboard actions
stories.description: As the operator, I want keyboard and clipboard behavior captured so workflows can reproduce shortcuts, copy-paste, and text-selection based extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Recorder captures key presses, hotkeys, copy, cut, paste, and text selection events with normalized key data, modifier flags, selection context, and clipboard linkage.
Persist clipboard-related events into a durable structure that later replay and output-mapping stories can reuse.
Show recorder-side indicators for the last shortcut captured and the current clipboard entry count during a recording session.
Example: `Control+C` on selected text followed by `Control+V` into another field records selection, copy, and paste steps in sequence.
Negative case: unsupported OS-level shortcuts outside the Chromium viewer are ignored and reported as out-of-scope rather than recorded inaccurately.

stories.title: Render and export the serialized workflow definition
stories.description: As the operator, I want to inspect and export recorded workflow data so I can validate what will later be replayed.
acceptanceCriteria: Render the ordered step list on `/workflows/:workflowId` with inspector panels for selector, payload, tab or frame context, timing, and optional assertions.
Add a raw JSON export or download action for the workflow definition using the persisted schema that replay will consume.
Support reordering and deleting steps in the editor without corrupting stable step IDs or tab references.
Example: selecting a recorded click step shows its selector strategy, target URL, and timing data in the inspector.
Negative case: if a stored step type is unknown to the current UI, render it as `unsupported` with safe fallback details instead of crashing the editor.

stories.title: Replay workflows in visible Chromium with speed controls
stories.description: As the operator, I want to replay a recorded workflow inside the visible Chromium browser so I can verify or reuse an automation path interactively.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement on-demand replay from a workflow definition in the visible Chromium session with run modes `normal`, `slow`, and `step`.
Persist a WorkflowRun record with status, start and end timestamps, mode, and final result placeholder, and stream live step status updates to the UI.
Make `/workflows/:workflowId/replay` show play controls, pause or step button states, active step highlighting, and current browser URL.
Example: replaying a workflow with navigate, click, and input steps runs them in order and marks the run `success` when the last step completes.
Negative case: starting replay without an available browser session or with an empty workflow is blocked with a clear validation message before any run record is created.

stories.title: Add waits, retries, and failure recovery entry points
stories.description: As the operator, I want replay timing controls and restart options so I can stabilize flaky workflows and recover after failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Support per-step wait strategies for selector presence, selector visibility, navigation completion, and fixed delay, plus configurable retry count and timeout defaults.
When a replay step fails, store the failure reason, attempt count, and failed step ID, then allow relaunching from the beginning or from the failed step.
Expose retry settings and recovery actions in `/workflows/:workflowId/replay` and `/runs/:runId` without requiring manual database edits.
Example: a step waiting for a delayed button succeeds on the second retry and the run continues automatically.
Negative case: when retries are exhausted because a selector never appears, the run stops, subsequent steps are marked `SKIP`, and the failure is clearly attributed to the missing selector.

stories.title: Capture, pre-inject, and mask cookies
stories.description: As the operator, I want cookie handling built into workflows so authenticated or stateful sessions can be reproduced safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist cookies observed during recording or replay with domain, path, expiry, secure and httpOnly flags, source step, and last updated timestamp.
Allow workflow configuration to define cookies for pre-injection before a run starts, including masked or secret values that are never echoed back in cleartext UI.
Apply injected cookies before the first navigation and refresh stored cookie artifacts when values change during a run.
Example: a workflow configured with a masked `session_id` cookie reaches an authenticated page on its first navigation.
Negative case: masked cookie values are hidden in tables, logs, exports, and debug payloads, showing only a redacted placeholder.

stories.title: Persist clipboard history and expose it in the run viewer
stories.description: As the operator, I want every clipboard value used during a run stored and viewable so it can drive output mapping and debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: Store every clipboard entry created or pasted during a run with sequence, MIME type, source action, source step, and captured timestamp.
Surface clipboard history in `/runs/:runId` and `/workflows/:workflowId` with filters for step, source action, and plain-text search.
Associate clipboard entries with workflow steps so later output templating can reference `clipboard[index]`, `clipboard.last`, and step-based lookups.
Example: a run that copies three order references displays all three entries in order with links back to the originating steps.
Negative case: when no clipboard event occurs during a run, the viewer shows an explicit empty state and the run still completes successfully.

stories.title: Generate structured JSON results and export actions
stories.description: As the operator, I want filtered clipboard data turned into structured JSON so workflow runs produce a usable output artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement workflow-level clipboard retention rules for keep or ignore by regex, MIME type, source, and step, then map the kept entries into a final JSON result using simple templates.
Support template tokens `{{clipboard[0]}}`, `{{clipboard.last}}`, and `{{step(<workflow-step-id>).clipboard}}` or an equivalent stable step reference, with validation before run start.
Make `/runs/:runId` provide actions to copy the final JSON to the clipboard and download it as a file.
Example: a workflow that copies an order ID and email can export `{ "orderId": "...", "email": "..." }` from the configured template.
Negative case: invalid template references or invalid regex syntax prevent the run from starting and show which token or rule is invalid.

stories.title: Schedule workflows and show run history
stories.description: As the operator, I want workflows to run on a schedule and keep a visible history so repetitive automations do not require manual launching.
acceptanceCriteria: Install scheduling support with the explicit command `npm install node-cron`.
Allow each workflow to run on a cron expression or fixed interval, store enabled or disabled state, and calculate `lastRunAt` and `nextRunAt`.
Show latest run status, last run time, next scheduled run, and paginated run history on the dashboard and workflow detail page.
Example: a workflow configured to run every 60 minutes shows the computed next run time immediately after being enabled.
Negative case: if a scheduled trigger fires while the workflow already has an active run, the new execution is skipped with status `skipped_busy` and a visible audit entry.

stories.title: Deliver the run timeline and automatic failure screenshots
stories.description: As the operator, I want a run viewer with per-step status and screenshot evidence so failures are immediately understandable.
acceptanceCriteria: Make `/runs/:runId` display a chronological step timeline with statuses `OK`, `FAIL`, and `SKIP`, plus attempted action, selector used, URL, frame, tab, timestamps, and elapsed time.
Capture a screenshot automatically when a step fails and attach it to the failed step details.
Show current cookies and clipboard artifacts in side panels while reviewing a run.
Example: a failed click step shows the exact selector used, the page URL, and a screenshot taken at the moment of failure.
Negative case: if screenshot capture itself fails, the run viewer shows a clear `screenshot unavailable` note without hiding the original automation error.

stories.title: Add HTML snapshots and browser console diagnostics
stories.description: As the operator, I want deeper diagnostics around a failed step so I can understand why a selector or page state diverged.
acceptanceCriteria: On step failure, persist a targeted DOM or HTML snapshot and browser console messages or errors alongside the screenshot and technical failure code.
Display failure reason categories such as `timeout`, `selector_not_found`, and `navigation_interrupted`, with raw diagnostic payloads accessible from an expandable panel.
Time-order console logs and tag them by level so users can correlate them with step timestamps.
Example: a missing-selector failure includes the rendered HTML around the lookup target and the console error that explains why page script execution broke.

Negative case: if DOM snapshot generation is blocked by a browser exception, the failure record still saves the original error and marks the snapshot field as unavailable instead of replacing the root cause.

stories.title: Support step-through debug, target highlighting, and resume
stories.description: As the operator, I want to debug a run interactively in the visible browser so I can inspect step-by-step behavior and recover faster.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide step-through execution controls that pause before each step, continue one step at a time, and resume normal playback from the current position.
When possible, highlight the target element in the visible browser before executing the active step and show the resolved selector in the debug panel.
Allow relaunching a run from the failed step using the last known cookies or session state when still valid, otherwise fall back to restart-from-beginning with an explanation.
Example: in `step` mode, the operator advances through two steps manually, sees the third target highlighted, and resumes automatic playback afterward.
Negative case: if highlighting is impossible because the target frame is cross-origin or detached, the UI explains that highlight is unavailable but still allows debug execution.

stories.title: Add smoke coverage and operator documentation
stories.description: As the maintainer, I want smoke coverage and operational documentation so the MVP is runnable, testable, and understandable from a fresh checkout.
acceptanceCriteria: Add automated smoke tests that cover workflow CRUD, visible browser session startup, record-to-replay happy path, cookie injection, clipboard result generation, and failure diagnostics at a minimal level.
Document local development, Docker execution, browser prerequisites, storage locations, and known MVP limitations in project docs and AGENTS operational notes.
Provide a sample workflow fixture that demonstrates multi-step navigation, clipboard capture, and JSON export for manual verification.
Example: a fresh clone can follow the documented steps to launch the stack with Docker and replay the sample workflow end-to-end.
Negative case: if Chromium binaries or display services are missing, the healthcheck and docs point to the exact failing prerequisite instead of failing silently.
